title: Crataegus × deltoxyacantha
taxonomy: Crataegus × deltoxyacantha is a hybrid hawthorn species, resulting from the cross between Crataegus monogyna and Crataegus laevigata. This hawthorn species is native to Europe. As a hybrid, it is often classified as a variety of Crataegus monogyna or Crataegus laevigata. However, some taxonomic systems consider it a separate species. The classification of Crataegus × deltoxyacantha can vary depending on the taxonomic system used.
uses: Crataegus × deltoxyacantha, a hybrid hawthorn species, has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to support cardiovascular health. Its fruit and leaves are rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may help lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could contribute to its potential therapeutic benefits. Some studies suggest that extracts from Crataegus × deltoxyacantha may help alleviate symptoms of heart failure and improve overall cardiovascular function. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using this plant for medicinal purposes.

properties: Crataegus × deltoxyacantha, a hawthorn hybrid, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential therapeutic properties, including lowering blood pressure and cholesterol levels due to its flavonoid content. intro: Crataegus × deltoxyacantha, also known as the hybrid hawthorn, is a deciduous shrub or small tree in the family Rosaceae. The health benefits of Crataegus × deltoxyacantha include its potential to improve cardiovascular health, reduce blood pressure, and lower cholesterol levels, making it a popular herbal remedy for heart-related issues. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, such as quercetin and epicatechin, as well as oligomeric proanthocyanidins (OPCs) and triterpenoids, which are thought to be responsible for its therapeutic effects. The most common herbal preparations made with Crataegus × deltoxyacantha include teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and reduce the risk of heart disease.
